title: About Me
paragraphs: Hi, I’m Rajiv. I am a Computer Science graduate from IIIT Guwahati and am currently working as a Software Engineer at Samsung R&D Institute India - Delhi.

I will be joining the University of California, Davis as a Master of Science student in Computer Science starting Fall 2026. My research interests include parallel computing, on-device AI, and Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) systems.

My core engineering and research interests lie at the intersection of parallel computing and IoT/embedded devices. I am highly focused on enhancing Machine Learning inference efficiency on constrained hardware, such as smart watches, TVs, and digital appliances. Throughout my career, I have built a strong foundation in on-device AI, embedded systems, and energy-efficient computing.

I have experience developing optimized AI systems and conducting research on high-performance computing workloads and GPU scheduling with IISc Bengaluru and IBM Research. Furthermore, I am deeply interested in Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) systems, having previously worked on EEG signal analysis and classification to build assistive technologies.
Outside of building software and reading research papers, my personal interests include bike riding through the mountains, weight lifting, and going on long car drives.
